title: agenui_catalog A2UI Catalog
description: agenui_catalog A2UI catalog, including standard_catalog, common_types.

text.description: The text content to display. While simple Markdown formatting is supported (i.e. without HTML, images, or links), utilizing dedicated UI components is generally preferred for a richer and more structured presentation.

variant.description: A hint for the base text style.

url.description: The URL of the image to display.

fit.description: Specifies how the image should be resized to fit its container. This corresponds to the CSS 'object-fit' property.

variant.description: A hint for the image size and style.

Icon.description: A first-class icon / glyph component. ALWAYS use this for any small icon, glyph, or icon-shaped placeholder (location pin, phone, calendar, star, etc.). Do NOT fake an icon with a sized Column or Card that has a grey background-color — that pattern is wrong even when no real icon URL is available; pick a sensible name from the 'name' enum below (e.g. 'locationOn' for an address row, 'phone' for a contact row, 'calendarToday' for a date row) and use this Icon component instead.

name.description: The name of the icon to display.

url.description: The URL of the video to display.
The URL of the audio to be played.

description.description: A description of the audio, such as a title or summary.

allOf.description: A layout component that arranges its children horizontally. To create a grid layout, nest Columns within this Row.

children.description: Defines the children. Use an array of strings for a fixed set of children, or a template object to generate children from a data list. Children cannot be defined inline, they must be referred to by ID.

justify.description: Defines the arrangement of children along the main axis (horizontally). Use 'spaceBetween' to push items to the edges, or 'start'/'end'/'center' to pack them together.

align.description: Defines the alignment of children along the cross axis (vertically). This is similar to the CSS 'align-items' property, but uses camelCase values (e.g., 'start').

allOf.description: A layout component that arranges its children vertically. To create a grid layout, nest Rows within this Column.

children.description: Defines the children. Use an array of strings for a fixed set of children, or a template object to generate children from a data list. Children cannot be defined inline, they must be referred to by ID.

justify.description: Defines the arrangement of children along the main axis (vertically). Use 'spaceBetween' to push items to the edges (e.g. header at top, footer at bottom), or 'start'/'end'/'center' to pack them together.

align.description: Defines the alignment of children along the cross axis (horizontally). This is similar to the CSS 'align-items' property.

children.description: Defines the children. Use an array of strings for a fixed set of children, or a template object to generate children from a data list.

direction.description: The direction in which the list items are laid out.

align.description: Defines the alignment of children along the cross axis.

child.description: The ID of the single child component to be rendered inside the card. To display multiple elements, you MUST wrap them in a layout component (like Column or Row) and pass that container's ID here. Do NOT pass multiple IDs or a non-existent ID. Do NOT define the child component inline.

tabs.description: An array of objects, where each object defines a tab with a title and a child component.

child.description: The ID of the child component. Do NOT define the component inline.

trigger.description: The ID of the component that opens the modal when interacted with (e.g., a button). Do NOT define the component inline.

content.description: The ID of the component to be displayed inside the modal. Do NOT define the component inline.

Divider.description: A first-class horizontal or vertical separator line. ALWAYS use this for any divider / separator / section break / 1px line. Do NOT fake a divider with an empty Column or Row that has height: 1px and a background-color — that pattern is wrong even though the schema accepts it; use this Divider component instead.

child.description: The ID of the child component. Use a 'Text' component for a labeled button. Only use an 'Icon' if the requirements explicitly ask for an icon-only button. Do NOT define the child component inline.

variant.description: A hint for the button style. If omitted, a default button style is used. 'primary' indicates this is the main call-to-action button. 'borderless' means the button has no visual border or background, making its child content appear like a clickable link.

label.description: The text label for the input field.

value.description: The value of the text field.

variant.description: The type of input field to display.

validationRegexp.description: A regular expression used for client-side validation of the input.

label.description: The text to display next to the checkbox.

value.description: The current state of the checkbox (true for checked, false for unchecked).

allOf.description: A component that allows selecting one or more options from a list.

label.description: The label for the group of options.

variant.description: A hint for how the choice picker should be displayed and behave.

options.description: The list of available options to choose from.

label.description: The text to display for this option.

value.description: The stable value associated with this option.
The list of currently selected values. This should be bound to a string array in the data model.

displayStyle.description: The display style of the component.

filterable.description: If true, displays a search input to filter the options.

min.description: The minimum value of the slider.

max.description: The maximum value of the slider.

value.description: The current value of the slider.
The selected date and/or time value in ISO 8601 format. If not yet set, initialize with an empty string.

enableDate.description: If true, allows the user to select a date.

enableTime.description: If true, allows the user to select a time.

min.description: The minimum allowed date/time in ISO 8601 format.

max.description: The maximum allowed date/time in ISO 8601 format.

label.description: The text label for the input field.

text.description:
Rich text content in HTML format. Supported tags include: , , ,
,
, , , , , , , , , . For more details, see: https://alidocs.dingtalk.com/i/nodes/Exel2BLV5P0p4n7ZCD555l0rWgk9rpMq

variant.description: A hint for the text style, used to set the base font size and style.

linksEnable.description: Whether to allow clicking on links. Defaults to true. When set to false, links will not be clickable.

url.description: The URL of the Lottie JSON animation file. Supports network URLs (http/https), local resources (res://animation_name), and local files (file:///path).

autoPlay.description: Whether to automatically play the animation. Defaults to true.

loop.description: Whether to loop the animation. Defaults to true.

variant.description: A hint for the animation size.

columns.description: Array of column header names. Defines the column titles of the table.

rows.description: Array of table data rows. Each row is a string array containing the data for each column in that row.

source.description: Supports complete URLs with http/https protocol, as well as HTML content. The WebView will automatically load and display the web content, supporting features such as JavaScript and adaptive height.

content.description: Text content in Markdown format. Implemented based on the Markwon library, supporting standard Markdown syntax: headings (# H1, ## H2), bold (**bold**), italic (*italic*), lists (- item), links ([text](url)), images (![alt](url)), code (`code`), quotes (> quote), tables, etc. Supports streaming incremental appending.

chartType.description: The type of chart to display. Supports bar chart, line chart, donut chart, and grouped bar chart.

data.description: Chart data configuration. The data structure varies depending on the chart type.

xAxis.description: X-axis data array. Used for horizontal axis labels in bar and line charts.

yAxis.description: Y-axis data array. Used to define vertical axis scale labels or categories.

series.description: Data series array. Each series represents a group of related data points.

name.description: Series name, used for legend display.

data.description: Data points array. Each data point contains a value and an optional label.

value.description: The numeric value of the data point.

label.description: The label of the data point, used to display additional information (such as time, description, etc.). For donut charts, label is used as the sector name.

content.description: An array of image URLs to display in the carousel.

autoplay.description: Whether the carousel should automatically cycle through images. Defaults to false.

draggable.description: Whether users can manually drag to switch between images. Defaults to true.

required.description: Checks that the value is not null, undefined, or empty.

value.description: The value to check.

regex.description: Checks that the value matches a regular expression string.

pattern.description: The regex pattern to match against.

email.description: Checks that the value is a valid email address.

formatString.description: Performs string interpolation of data model values and other functions in the catalog functions list and returns the resulting string. The value string can contain interpolated expressions in the `${expression}` format. Supported expression types include: JSON Pointer paths to the data model (e.g., `${/absolute/path}` or `${relative/path}`), and client-side function calls (e.g., `${now()}`). Function arguments must be named (e.g., `${formatDate(value:${/currentDate}, format:'MM-dd')}`). To include a literal `${` sequence, escape it as `\${`.

formatNumber.description: Formats a number with the specified grouping and decimal precision.

value.description: The number to format.

decimals.description: Optional. The number of decimal places to show. Defaults to 0 or 2 depending on locale.

grouping.description: Optional. If true, uses locale-specific grouping separators (e.g. '1,000'). If false, returns raw digits (e.g. '1000'). Defaults to true.

formatCurrency.description: Formats a number as a currency string.

value.description: The monetary amount.

decimals.description: Optional. The number of decimal places to show. Defaults to 0 or 2 depending on locale.

grouping.description: Optional. If true, uses locale-specific grouping separators (e.g. '1,000'). If false, returns raw digits (e.g. '1000'). Defaults to true.

formatDate.description: Formats a timestamp into a string using a pattern.

value.description: The date to format.

format.description:
A Unicode TR35 date pattern string.

Token Reference:
- Year: 'yy' (26), 'yyyy' (2026)
- Month: 'M' (1), 'MM' (01), 'MMM' (Jan), 'MMMM' (January)
- Day: 'd' (1), 'dd' (01), 'E' (Tue), 'EEEE' (Tuesday)
- Hour (12h): 'h' (1-12), 'hh' (01-12) - requires 'a' for AM/PM
- Hour (24h): 'H' (0-23), 'HH' (00-23) - Military Time
- Minute: 'mm' (00-59)
- Second: 'ss' (00-59)
- Period: 'a' (AM/PM)

Examples:
- 'MMM dd, yyyy' -> 'Jan 16, 2026'
- 'HH:mm' -> '14:30' (Military)
- 'h:mm a' -> '2:30 PM'
- 'EEEE, d MMMM' -> 'Friday, 16 January'

pluralize.description: Returns a localized string based on the Common Locale Data Repository (CLDR) plural category of the count (zero, one, two, few, many, other). Requires an 'other' fallback. For English, just use 'one' and 'other'.

value.description: The numeric value used to determine the plural category.

two.description: String for the 'two' category (used in Arabic, Welsh, etc.).

few.description: String for the 'few' category (e.g., small groups in Slavic languages).

many.description: String for the 'many' category (e.g., large groups in various languages).

other.description: The default/fallback string (used for general plural cases).

openUrl.description: Opens the specified URL in a browser or handler. This function has no return value.

and.description: Performs a logical AND operation on a list of boolean values.

values.description: The list of boolean values to evaluate.

or.description: Performs a logical OR operation on a list of boolean values.

values.description: The list of boolean values to evaluate.

not.description: Performs a logical NOT operation on a boolean value.

value.description: The boolean value to negate.

common_types_ComponentId.description: The unique identifier for a component, used for both definitions and references within the same surface.

path.description: A JSON Pointer path to a value in the data model.

common_types_DynamicValue.description: A value that can be a literal, a path, or a function call returning any type.

common_types_FunctionCall.description: Invokes a named function on the client.

call.description: The name of the function to call.

returnType.description: The expected return type of the function call.

common_types_AccessibilityAttributes.description: Attributes to enhance accessibility when using assistive technologies like screen readers.

label.description: A short string, typically 1 to 3 words, used by assistive technologies to convey the purpose or intent of an element. For example, an input field might have an accessible label of 'User ID' or a button might be labeled 'Submit'.

description.description: Additional information provided by assistive technologies about an element such as instructions, format requirements, or result of an action. For example, a mute button might have a label of 'Mute' and a description of 'Silences notifications about this conversation'.

common_types_Styles.description: Style property definitions for controlling component appearance and layout. Position and dimension properties support px units and percentages.

width.description: Width. Supports two formats: 1) px value, e.g., 100px 2) percentage of parent width, e.g., 100% or 50.5%. Use 100% to make the component fill its parent's width.

height.description: Height. Supports two formats: 1) px value, e.g., 100px 2) percentage of parent height, e.g., 100% or 50.5%.

margin.description: Margin, only supports four-value shorthand form. Format: top right bottom left, e.g., 10px 20px 10px 20px

margin-inline-start.description: Inline start margin (left side in LTR layout, right side in RTL layout). Format: number+px, e.g., 10px

margin-inline-end.description: Inline end margin (right side in LTR layout, left side in RTL layout). Format: number+px, e.g., 10px

margin-block-start.description: Block start margin (typically top side). Format: number+px, e.g., 10px

margin-block-end.description: Block end margin (typically bottom side). Format: number+px, e.g., 10px

padding.description: Padding, only supports four-value shorthand form. Format: top right bottom left, e.g., 10px 20px 10px 20px

padding-inline-start.description: Inline start padding (left side in LTR layout, right side in RTL layout). Format: number+px, e.g., 10px

padding-inline-end.description: Inline end padding (right side in LTR layout, left side in RTL layout). Format: number+px, e.g., 10px

padding-block-start.description: Block start padding (typically top side). Format: number+px, e.g., 10px

padding-block-end.description: Block end padding (typically bottom side). Format: number+px, e.g., 10px

align-self.description: Flexbox individual item alignment, overrides align-items

flex-grow.description: Flexbox grow factor, defaults to 0 (no growth)

flex-shrink.description: Flexbox shrink factor, defaults to 1 (shrinks when space is insufficient)

flex-basis.description: Flexbox initial main size. Supports three formats: 1) px value, e.g., 100px 2) percentage of parent, e.g., 50% 3) auto (sized by content or width/height)

background-color.description: Background color. Supported formats: 1) 6-digit hex #ff0000, 2) 8-digit hex (with alpha) #ff000080, 3) RGB rgb(255, 0, 0), 4) RGBA rgba(255, 99, 71, 0.5)

border-color.description: Border color. Supported formats: 1) 6-digit hex #ff0000, 2) 8-digit hex (with alpha) #ff000080, 3) RGB rgb(255, 0, 0), 4) RGBA rgba(255, 99, 71, 0.5)

border-style.description: Border style, only supports solid (solid line)

opacity.description: Opacity, value range 0-1. 0 is fully transparent, 1 is fully opaque

overflow.description: Overflow content handling. hidden: hide overflow content, visible: show overflow content

position.description: Positioning mode. relative: offset from normal position; absolute: positioned relative to nearest positioned ancestor

top.description: Top offset. Supports two formats: 1) px value, e.g., 10px 2) percentage of parent height, e.g., 50%

left.description: Left offset. Supports two formats: 1) px value, e.g., 10px 2) percentage of parent width, e.g., 50%

right.description: Right offset. Supports two formats: 1) px value, e.g., 10px 2) percentage of parent width, e.g., 50%

bottom.description: Bottom offset. Supports two formats: 1) px value, e.g., 10px 2) percentage of parent height, e.g., 50%

display.description: Display mode, only supports none (hide element)

visibility.description: Visibility. visible: visible, hidden: invisible but occupies space

filter.description: CSS filter effects, only supports drop-shadow. Format: drop-shadow(offset-x offset-y blur-radius color), e.g., drop-shadow(0px 4px 4px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.25))

aspect-ratio.description: CSS aspect ratio property. Supports two formats: 1) Single number (e.g., 1.777, 1.333, 1); 2) Ratio format (e.g., 16 / 9, 4 / 3, 1 / 1)

background-image.description: Background image using CSS url() function syntax (unquoted format only). Example: url(https://example.com/image.png). Supports http/https URLs

common_types_TextStyles.description: Style property definitions specific to text components, including all common style properties and text-specific style properties.

line-clamp.description: Maximum number of lines to display. When value is less than or equal to 0, it means unlimited; when value is greater than 0, text exceeding the line count will be truncated. Must be used with text-overflow to show ellipsis effect

oneOf.description: Line height multiplier, e.g., 0.5 means 0.5 times normal line height, 1.5 means 1.5 times
Specified line height value, only supports px unit, e.g., 10px. Note: px values are not supported in line-wrapping scenarios, must use line height multiplier

line-height.description: Line height setting. Supports two formats: 1) Numeric multiplier (e.g., 0.5, 1.5) 2) px value (e.g., 10px, but not supported in line-wrapping scenarios, must use multiplier)

text-overflow.description: Text overflow handling, must be used with line-clamp. clip: default value, truncate text; head: ellipsis at the beginning (requires line-clamp to be 1, supported in 855+); middle: ellipsis in the middle (requires line-clamp to be 1, supported in 855+); ellipsis: ellipsis at the end (treated as clip if line-clamp is not 1)

text-align.description: Text alignment. left top: top-left corner; left/left center: left side vertically centered; left bottom: bottom-left corner; center top: horizontally centered top-aligned; center/center center: horizontally and vertically centered; center bottom: horizontally centered bottom-aligned; right top: top-right corner; right/right center: vertically centered right-aligned; right bottom: bottom-right corner

color.description: Text color. Supported formats: 1) 6-digit hex #ff0000, 2) 8-digit hex (with alpha) #ff000080, 3) RGB rgb(255, 0, 0), 4) RGBA rgba(255, 99, 71, 0.5)

font-size.description: Font size, only supports px unit. Format: number+px, e.g., 16px

font-weight.description: Font weight. Android only supports bold, other values will be treated as normal

font-family.description: Font family name, e.g., Arial, sans-serif

text-decoration.description: Text decoration combined property. Format: [line] [style] [color], e.g., underline dashed #FF0000. line: underline or line-through; style: solid or dashed; color: hexadecimal color

text-decoration-line.description: Decoration line type. underline: underline; line-through: strikethrough

text-decoration-thickness.description: Decoration line thickness, only supports px unit. Format: number+px, e.g., 2px

text-decoration-style.description: Decoration line style. solid: solid line; dashed: dashed line

text-decoration-color.description: Decoration line color, only supports hexadecimal format. Format: #RRGGBB or #RRGGBBAA, e.g., #FF0000 or #FF0000FF

standard_catalog_TextComponentCommon.description: Common properties for Text component, with extended text-specific styles.

styles.description: Style properties for the Text component, including common styles and text-specific styles.

weight.description: The relative weight of this component within a Row or Column. This is similar to the CSS 'flex-grow' property. Note: this may ONLY be set when the component is a direct descendant of a Row or Column.

oneOf.description: A static list of child component IDs.
A template for generating a dynamic list of children from a data model list. The `componentId` is the component to use as a template.

path.description: The path to the list of component property objects in the data model.

common_types_DynamicBoolean.description: A boolean value that can be a literal, a path, or a function call returning a boolean.

common_types_CheckRule.description: A single validation rule applied to an input component.

message.description: The error message to display if the check fails.

common_types_Checkable.description: Properties for components that support client-side checks.

checks.description: A list of checks to perform. These are function calls that must return a boolean indicating validity.

common_types_Action.description: Defines an interaction handler that can either trigger a server-side event or execute a local client-side function.

event.description: The event to dispatch to the server.

name.description: The name of the action to be dispatched to the server.

context.description: A JSON object containing the key-value pairs for the action context. Values can be literals or paths. Use literal values unless the value must be dynamically bound to the data model. Do NOT use paths for static IDs.

common_types_DynamicStringList.description: Represents a value that can be either a literal array of strings, a path to a string array in the data model, or a function call returning a string array.

common_types_DynamicNumber.description: Represents a value that can be either a literal number, a path to a number in the data model, or a function call returning a number.

text.description: The text content to display. While simple Markdown formatting is supported (i.e. without HTML, images, or links), utilizing dedicated UI components is generally preferred for a richer and more structured presentation.

variant.description: A hint for the base text style.

url.description: The URL of the image to display.

fit.description: Specifies how the image should be resized to fit its container. This corresponds to the CSS 'object-fit' property.

variant.description: A hint for the image size and style.

standard_catalog_regex.description: Checks that the value matches a regular expression string.

pattern.description: The regex pattern to match against.

standard_catalog_email.description: Checks that the value is a valid email address.

standard_catalog_formatString.description: Performs string interpolation of data model values and other functions in the catalog functions list and returns the resulting string. The value string can contain interpolated expressions in the `${expression}` format. Supported expression types include: JSON Pointer paths to the data model (e.g., `${/absolute/path}` or `${relative/path}`), and client-side function calls (e.g., `${now()}`). Function arguments must be named (e.g., `${formatDate(value:${/currentDate}, format:'MM-dd')}`). To include a literal `${` sequence, escape it as `\${`.

standard_catalog_formatNumber.description: Formats a number with the specified grouping and decimal precision.

value.description: The number to format.

decimals.description: Optional. The number of decimal places to show. Defaults to 0 or 2 depending on locale.

grouping.description: Optional. If true, uses locale-specific grouping separators (e.g. '1,000'). If false, returns raw digits (e.g. '1000'). Defaults to true.

standard_catalog_formatCurrency.description: Formats a number as a currency string.

value.description: The monetary amount.

decimals.description: Optional. The number of decimal places to show. Defaults to 0 or 2 depending on locale.

grouping.description: Optional. If true, uses locale-specific grouping separators (e.g. '1,000'). If false, returns raw digits (e.g. '1000'). Defaults to true.

standard_catalog_formatDate.description: Formats a timestamp into a string using a pattern.

value.description: The date to format.

format.description:
A Unicode TR35 date pattern string.

Token Reference:
- Year: 'yy' (26), 'yyyy' (2026)
- Month: 'M' (1), 'MM' (01), 'MMM' (Jan), 'MMMM' (January)
- Day: 'd' (1), 'dd' (01), 'E' (Tue), 'EEEE' (Tuesday)
- Hour (12h): 'h' (1-12), 'hh' (01-12) - requires 'a' for AM/PM
- Hour (24h): 'H' (0-23), 'HH' (00-23) - Military Time
- Minute: 'mm' (00-59)
- Second: 'ss' (00-59)
- Period: 'a' (AM/PM)

Examples:
- 'MMM dd, yyyy' -> 'Jan 16, 2026'
- 'HH:mm' -> '14:30' (Military)
- 'h:mm a' -> '2:30 PM'
- 'EEEE, d MMMM' -> 'Friday, 16 January'

standard_catalog_pluralize.description: Returns a localized string based on the Common Locale Data Repository (CLDR) plural category of the count (zero, one, two, few, many, other). Requires an 'other' fallback. For English, just use 'one' and 'other'.

value.description: The numeric value used to determine the plural category.

two.description: String for the 'two' category (used in Arabic, Welsh, etc.).

few.description: String for the 'few' category (e.g., small groups in Slavic languages).

many.description: String for the 'many' category (e.g., large groups in various languages).

other.description: The default/fallback string (used for general plural cases).

standard_catalog_openUrl.description: Opens the specified URL in a browser or handler. This function has no return value.

standard_catalog_and.description: Performs a logical AND operation on a list of boolean values.

values.description: The list of boolean values to evaluate.

standard_catalog_or.description: Performs a logical OR operation on a list of boolean values.

values.description: The list of boolean values to evaluate.

standard_catalog_not.description: Performs a logical NOT operation on a boolean value.

value.description: The boolean value to negate.

primaryColor.description: The primary brand color used for highlights (e.g., primary buttons, active borders). Renderers may generate variants of this color for different contexts. Format: Hexadecimal code (e.g., '#00BFFF').

iconUrl.description: A URL for an image that identifies the agent or tool associated with the surface.

agentDisplayName.description: Text to be displayed next to the surface to identify the agent or tool that created it.
